arts.ttl: “We’re doing our best” – Club in a hurry to sign Fulham player, Manchester City could help
arts.sum: Manchester City could help Palmeiras in their ongoing pursuit of Fulham’s Andreas Pereira. Earlier today, we covered claims from Brazil which stated Palmeiras have accepted a bid tabled by Manchester City for Vitor Reis. Manchester City’s offer is said to be around €30-35m and Palmeiras can use some of it to sign the Fulham player. arts.ttl: West Ham’s striker obsession remains unresolved after years of failure | Jacob Steinberg
arts.sum: Graham Potter must quickly get to grips with the club’s failure to sign the right centre-forward over the past 15 years West Ham rarely hit the target when they buy a striker. Names such as Mido, Ilan, Benni McCarthy, Jonathan Calleri and Simone Zaza will send a shiver down the spine of Hammers supporters, who must despair at the same old mistakes being made over and over again. This has been a constant theme since David Sullivan and the late David Gold bought the club in January 2010. The 15-year anniversary arrives on Sunday and the numbers have not improved in the eight years since I first wrote about West Ham’s striking failure, detailing how the 32 forwards bought during Sullivan and Gold’s ownership at that point had mustered 128 goals in 643 appearances. Continue reading...
